articles.title: DealFlow Events Announces PREDICT: The Prediction Markets Conference(TM)
articles.summary: DealFlow Events is launching PREDICT, a dedicated conference for the prediction markets industry. The event signals growing institutional interest in prediction markets as they transition from niche betting platforms to legitimate forecasting tools for finance and policy.

articles.title: Crypto Prediction Markets: Limitations and Risks for Traders in 2026 - News and Statistics
articles.summary: Crypto prediction markets face growing scrutiny over liquidity constraints, regulatory uncertainty, and volatility risks that could hammer trader profits through 2026. Smart money is already hedging bets across traditional platforms, knowing that crypto market swings can turn even solid predictions into costly disasters.

articles.title: NCSL Town Hall: Prediction Markets
articles.summary: The National Conference of State Legislatures held a town hall focused on prediction markets, likely discussing regulatory frameworks and legislative approaches across different states. This signals growing mainstream political attention to prediction markets, which could accelerate state-level legalization efforts or create new regulatory hurdles depending on how lawmakers frame the discussion.

articles.title: Masters action shows ongoing sports-betting growth of prediction markets
articles.summary: The Masters tournament is driving increased activity in sports prediction markets, signaling another step in their mainstream adoption beyond politics and finance. Golf's prestige audience and week-long format create perfect conditions for sophisticated bettors to test prediction markets against traditional sportsbooks, potentially accelerating the shift toward more efficient pricing mechanisms.

articles.title: Dodgers Dominate Kalshi's NL Champion Market, But One Unlikely Team Is Gaining Ground
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Dodgers are crushing the National League Championship odds on Kalshi while some dark horse team is quietly climbing the board. This creates a classic prediction market setup where sharp money might be finding value in the longshot while casual bettors pile onto the obvious favorite.

articles.title: Prediction Market Making Is Hard
articles.summary: Market makers are struggling to price prediction markets accurately, getting burned by information asymmetries and volatile swings that don't follow traditional financial patterns. This creates huge opportunities for informed bettors to exploit mispriced contracts, but also threatens the liquidity that makes these markets function in the first place.

articles.title: ‘Cowboy’: Michael Selig, a top Trump Wall Street regulator, comes out swinging
articles.summary: Michael Selig, a key Trump-era Wall Street regulator, is making aggressive moves that signal a major shift in financial oversight policy. His confrontational approach could reshape market regulations and create new volatility in financial sector stocks, making regulatory policy bets suddenly very interesting.

articles.title: CFTC sues states to assert exclusive jurisdiction over prediction markets
articles.summary: The CFTC is taking states to court to establish that only federal regulators can oversee prediction markets, not state gambling commissions or attorneys general. This legal battle will determine whether prediction markets face a patchwork of conflicting state rules or operate under unified federal oversight, directly impacting which platforms can launch and where Americans can legally bet on elections.

articles.title: Only 50/50 odds that the Strait of Hormuz normalizes by July according to prediction markets
articles.summary: Prediction markets are pricing just 50% odds that shipping through the Strait of Hormuz returns to normal operations by July, suggesting traders expect prolonged disruption to the world's most critical oil chokepoint. Smart money is betting that geopolitical tensions will keep tanker traffic snarled for months, creating massive ripple effects across energy prices and global supply chains that savvy bettors can capitalize on.

articles.title: Prediction markets have exploded. What can they tell us about Oregon’s hottest primary race? - Oregon Public Broadcasting
articles.summary: Oregon Public Broadcasting is covering how prediction markets are being used to handicap the state's most competitive primary election. It signals that mainstream media outlets are finally treating prediction markets as legitimate political forecasting tools worth covering alongside traditional polling.

articles.title: Kalshi Is Half Right About Prediction Markets and Gambling
articles.summary: Kalshi's making their case that prediction markets aren't gambling, but they're only getting part of the argument right. Getting the distinction wrong could determine whether prediction markets stay legal or get lumped in with sports betting regulations.

articles.title: Google makes a 'betting mistake' with Polymarkets, calls it a …
articles.summary: Google apparently made some kind of error or misjudgment involving Polymarket and labeled the platform something unflattering. This matters because when tech giants publicly comment on prediction markets, it shapes mainstream perception and potentially influences regulatory attitudes toward the entire industry.
